feature: M3 §1 — scripts/scan.mjs: a new deterministic compiler-side deep-scan script that walks a repo's danger surfaces and emits scan-notes.md (surface → file:line evidence → nominated roster expert/rule), then STOPS for owner review. Bounded (--max), resumable (frontmatter scanned_paths cursor), scan-only (never executes scanned content). First buildable slice of M3; downstream mine.mjs (§2) consumes its output. Spec at .claude/veriloop/specs/m3-scan.md (binding); authority is docs/plans/m3-plan.md §1.

checks.name: test
checks.tail:
scan: a second run adds NO duplicate surface headers (header count === unique count)
scan: a second run adds NO new surface blocks at all (cursor skips completed paths)

224 ok, 0 failed

concerns: [Baseline Reviewer (correctness & craft) — MODE: REVIEW] `--max` silently and PERMANENTLY drops surfaces, contradicting the spec's framing of BOUNDED + RESUMABLE as complementary. Every invocation walks the WHOLE tree and marks ALL walked paths into the cursor (line 257), regardless of how many surface blocks `--max` allowed. Because resumption is keyed on scanned PATHS, not on un-emitted surfaces, a capped run can never be resumed to emit the rest — the excess surfaces are lost forever. Demonstrated: `scan --repo . --out f --max 2` emits 2 headers and marks all 73 paths scanned; a second identical run stays at 2 headers (the other ~4 surfaces are gone, not deferred). The spec says '--max cap per invocation' + 're-run skips completed paths', which reads as 'run again to get the rest' — but that is impossible here. Latent under the default (max 12 > 7 catalog entries) but reachable with any `--max` below the number of hit surfaces.

[Baseline Reviewer (correctness & craft) — MODE: REVIEW] The per-surface 12-evidence cap combined with the alphabetical walk and scanning of markdown/docs evicts real code evidence in favor of prose mentions. Scanning veriloop itself, the `shell-string execution` block's 12 slots fill from CHANGELOG.md:4, docs/plans/m3-plan.md (x4), fixtures/scan-target/run-check.mjs, generate.mjs, lint-bundle.mjs, scan.mjs:56 (its own catalog regex), and selftest.mjs — all doc/self-referential mentions of the pattern string, not spawn sites. The spec's own named smoke-test surface, `verify.mjs:64 shell:true`, is therefore NOT cited as evidence anywhere (verify.mjs appears only in the scanned_paths cursor at output line 74). The surface block exists, but the flagship real code hit is dropped, and the noisy citations are exactly what mine.mjs (§2) will consume downstream.

[security (Supply-Chain & Input-Safety Reviewer) — MODE: REVIEW] BOUNDED (--max) and RESUMABLE (path cursor) — two spec-required features — interact to PERMANENTLY DROP nominated danger surfaces. When --max caps the number of emitted surface blocks in a run, the cursor merge at :255-257 still marks EVERY walked path as scanned. On the next run, `fresh` (:238) is empty, collectEvidence returns nothing, and the surfaces that were capped out at :247 (`if (emitted >= args.max) break`) can never be emitted — the owner never sees them. Reproduced: `--max 2` on fixtures/scan-target emits shell + secret surfaces, marks all 3 paths scanned; a second run (no --max) emits 0 and the `parity/golden-fixture` surface (golden.fixture.json) is lost forever. Security impact: the whole point of the deep scan is not to MISS a danger surface, and a bounded run silently discards them instead of deferring to a resumable re-run. Only hidden by the default --max 12 exceeding the 7-entry catalog, so it never fires by default — but the bound is broken the moment it is exercised.

[security (Supply-Chain & Input-Safety Reviewer) — MODE: REVIEW] The plan's shipped implementation-note claims 'pointed at veriloop itself it surfaces verify.mjs shell: true', but MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_SURFACE=12 (:156) combined with the alphabetical walk fills the shell-string surface's 12 evidence slots with prose/doc false-positives (CHANGELOG.md:4, docs/plans/m3-plan.md:72/89/126/222 — markdown literally containing the text 'shell:') and fixtures, crowding out the REAL production hit scripts/verify.mjs:64 (`shell: true`, confirmed present). The shell-string SURFACE is still nominated, so no surface is missed, but verify.mjs:64 is NOT among the cited evidence — so the plan's factual claim is now false (CLAUDE.md rule 6: existing docs must stay truthful), and for a security tool, prose/self-fixture matches consuming the evidence budget ahead of real code sites is a precision regression.

[drift (Drift Sentinel — REVIEW mode)] BOUNDED (--max) and RESUMABLE (scanned_paths cursor) are mutually destructive. The emit loop stops after --max NEW surface blocks (line 247), but the cursor merge unconditionally marks EVERY walked path as scanned (line 257: `for (const p of allPaths) scannedPaths.add(...)`). On a re-run, `fresh` (line 238) is empty because all paths are in the cursor, so any surface that was capped out on the first invocation can never be emitted — it is dropped permanently, not deferred. The spec lists both BOUNDED and RESUMABLE as first-class required behaviors; their intended combination is incremental progress across invocations, not silent permanent loss. Reproduced against veriloop itself: `scan --repo . --out /tmp/vlm.md --max 2` emits 2 surfaces and writes 73 paths to the cursor; a follow-up `--max 12` run reports `0 new this run` and the 3 remaining real surfaces (DB/SQL/RLS, filesystem writes, parity/golden) are never emitted. Latent under the default (max 12 > 7 catalog entries) so the acceptance path passes, but --max is a data-loss footgun in exactly the scenario it exists for.
implSummary:
Implemented M3 §1 — scripts/scan.mjs, the deterministic phase-3 deep scan, exactly to the binding spec: scan-and-stop only, no mining/scoring/execution.

scripts/scan.mjs (new, compiler-side, NOT emitted): CLI mirrors detect.mjs (--repo/--out/--max default 12/--help, reusing the reqVal shape). A hardcoded danger-surface CATALOG of { name, expert, rule, line?/path? matcher, uiOnly? } matched IN PROCESS (regex over file text and/or relative path) — minimum catalog per spec: shell-string exec, secret/env, DB/SQL/RLS, untrusted-input→sink, fs-writes/machine-owned-emission (splice markers), parity/golden-fixture, and a UI-rendering surface gated on a cheap self-contained package.json-deps UI check. Every expert key is one of SPECIALIST_DEFAULTS (security|drift|ux) so a nomination maps 1:1 onto applyRosterAdd. Sorted recursive walk (stable output) skipping node_modules/.git/.claude/veriloop/.backups; reads files as TEXT only and spawns NOTHING (SCAN-ONLY covenant; git-history mining stays in §2). Emits scan-notes.md — scanned_paths: frontmatter cursor + one `## surface:` block per hit with real file:line evidence + `nominates: expert=<key> | rule="..."`. BOUNDED (--max new surfaces/invocation) and RESUMABLE (re-run skips completed paths, adds no duplicate headers, preserves owner-reviewed body byte-for-byte). CLASSIFICATION-CONFIRM HALT: writes, prints `review scan-notes.md, then run mine.mjs`, exits 0 — never chains to mining, never runs/compiles a nominated check. Smoke-tested against veriloop itself: surfaces verify.mjs shell:true, .gitignore splice markers, golden-fixture surfaces.

fixtures/scan-target/ (new, INPUT ONLY, no pre-seeded scan-notes.md): run-check.mjs (shell:true), config.mjs (process.env.FOO_KEY + SERVICE_ROLE), golden.fixture.json.

selftest: added a scan block (+12 assertions, 212→224) asserting (a) expected surface blocks emitted, (b) each nomination cites a real in-range file:line + valid expert key, (c) a second run adds no duplicate/new headers (writes --out to an mkdtemp path never inside fixtures/), (d) scan classified run-check.mjs rather than executing it — interrogating scan's DECISION per the ci-adopt rule-3 discipline.

Version bumped 0.3.7→0.3.8 (patch, not the 0.4.0 milestone stamp) across all six locations; version-stamp-agreement selftest green. Docs: appended an implementation-notes paragraph to docs/plans/m3-plan.md §1; SKILL.md:103 scan-notes.md reference confirmed truthful (no change). No changes to detect/verify/generate/lint-bundle behavior; no LLM in scan.mjs. NOT committed/pushed/reviewed per instructions.

lenses.summary: No BLOCKERs. The scan-only covenant, no-LLM discipline, six-stamp version agreement, manifest exclusion, lint-bundle exit 0, and selftest (224 > 212, green) all hold; the fixture selftest correctly interrogates scan's decision without executing fixture content. Two SHOULD-FIX correctness/precision issues: (1) `--max` permanently drops surfaces rather than deferring them because the cursor advances over the whole tree every run — bounded and resumable are wired to contradict each other (latent under default max=12, reachable with a lower --max); (2) the 12-evidence cap plus alphabetical walk plus scanning docs evicts real code evidence with doc/self-referential prose mentions, so the spec's own named smoke-test surface verify.mjs:64 shell:true is never cited when scanning veriloop itself, polluting what mine.mjs will consume. One NIT on a colliding magic constant.

findings.issue: `--max` silently and PERMANENTLY drops surfaces, contradicting the spec's framing of BOUNDED + RESUMABLE as complementary. Every invocation walks the WHOLE tree and marks ALL walked paths into the cursor (line 257), regardless of how many surface blocks `--max` allowed. Because resumption is keyed on scanned PATHS, not on un-emitted surfaces, a capped run can never be resumed to emit the rest — the excess surfaces are lost forever. Demonstrated: `scan --repo . --out f --max 2` emits 2 headers and marks all 73 paths scanned; a second identical run stays at 2 headers (the other ~4 surfaces are gone, not deferred). The spec says '--max cap per invocation' + 're-run skips completed paths', which reads as 'run again to get the rest' — but that is impossible here. Latent under the default (max 12 > 7 catalog entries) but reachable with any `--max` below the number of hit surfaces.
findings.fix: Only advance the cursor for paths actually consumed toward emitted surfaces, OR (simpler) do not mark paths scanned when `emitted >= args.max` truncated output — persist scanned paths only up to the point the bound was hit so a re-run can emit the deferred surfaces. Alternatively, if --max is meant purely as a hard per-run ceiling with no deferral, document that capped surfaces are dropped, not resumable, and warn on stderr when truncation occurs.

findings.issue: The per-surface 12-evidence cap combined with the alphabetical walk and scanning of markdown/docs evicts real code evidence in favor of prose mentions. Scanning veriloop itself, the `shell-string execution` block's 12 slots fill from CHANGELOG.md:4, docs/plans/m3-plan.md (x4), fixtures/scan-target/run-check.mjs, generate.mjs, lint-bundle.mjs, scan.mjs:56 (its own catalog regex), and selftest.mjs — all doc/self-referential mentions of the pattern string, not spawn sites. The spec's own named smoke-test surface, `verify.mjs:64 shell:true`, is therefore NOT cited as evidence anywhere (verify.mjs appears only in the scanned_paths cursor at output line 74). The surface block exists, but the flagship real code hit is dropped, and the noisy citations are exactly what mine.mjs (§2) will consume downstream.
findings.fix: Bias evidence selection toward higher-signal hits before truncating: e.g. exclude `.md`/doc files from the code-pattern (`line`) matchers, or rank real spawn/code hits above prose mentions, or raise/lift the per-surface cap so known surfaces like verify.mjs:64 survive. At minimum, ensure the owner-named smoke-test surfaces (verify.mjs:64 shell:true) actually appear in a self-scan so the catalog demonstrably 'finds veriloop's OWN known surfaces' as the spec requires.

findings.issue: MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_SURFACE is hardcoded to 12, coincidentally identical to the default `--max` (surface cap). Two unrelated bounds sharing the literal 12 invites confusion when tuning either.
findings.fix: Give the constant a value distinct from the --max default, or add a one-line comment clarifying these are independent axes (evidence-per-surface vs. surfaces-per-run).

lenses.summary: No BLOCKERs. The core security property this milestone must not weaken — the scan-only covenant (constitution rules 4/5) — is upheld: scan.mjs imports only node:fs/node:path, spawns nothing, and reads all scanned content (including hostile fixtures) as TEXT; verified by grep and by the self-scan classifying run-check.mjs without executing it. Emitted-artifact hygiene (rule 7) holds (relative paths only; scan.mjs not emitted). Selftest green (224, up from 212), lint-bundle exit 0, six version stamps agree at 0.3.8, and the acceptance run reproduces (real :line citations, no duplicate headers on re-run). Two SHOULD-FIXes: (1) a latent data-loss bug where --max (bounded) plus the path cursor (resumable) permanently drops capped-out danger surfaces — reproduced with --max 2, hidden only because default max 12 > 7 catalog entries; (2) the 12-evidence cap plus alphabetical walk lets markdown prose false-positives crowd real production hits (verify.mjs:64 shell:true) out of the cited evidence, making the plan's shipped implementation-note inaccurate. One NIT: manifest veriloop_version stale at 0.3.7.

findings.issue: BOUNDED (--max) and RESUMABLE (path cursor) — two spec-required features — interact to PERMANENTLY DROP nominated danger surfaces. When --max caps the number of emitted surface blocks in a run, the cursor merge at :255-257 still marks EVERY walked path as scanned. On the next run, `fresh` (:238) is empty, collectEvidence returns nothing, and the surfaces that were capped out at :247 (`if (emitted >= args.max) break`) can never be emitted — the owner never sees them. Reproduced: `--max 2` on fixtures/scan-target emits shell + secret surfaces, marks all 3 paths scanned; a second run (no --max) emits 0 and the `parity/golden-fixture` surface (golden.fixture.json) is lost forever. Security impact: the whole point of the deep scan is not to MISS a danger surface, and a bounded run silently discards them instead of deferring to a resumable re-run. Only hidden by the default --max 12 exceeding the 7-entry catalog, so it never fires by default — but the bound is broken the moment it is exercised.
findings.fix: Make the cursor surface-aware, not just path-aware: when the --max cap is hit (more surfaces had hits than were emitted), do NOT advance the path cursor to include paths whose surfaces were deferred — or track emitted-vs-pending surfaces so a re-run re-scans and emits the deferred ones. Add a selftest asserting a low --max run followed by a re-run eventually emits ALL surfaces.

findings.issue: The plan's shipped implementation-note claims 'pointed at veriloop itself it surfaces verify.mjs shell: true', but MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_SURFACE=12 (:156) combined with the alphabetical walk fills the shell-string surface's 12 evidence slots with prose/doc false-positives (CHANGELOG.md:4, docs/plans/m3-plan.md:72/89/126/222 — markdown literally containing the text 'shell:') and fixtures, crowding out the REAL production hit scripts/verify.mjs:64 (`shell: true`, confirmed present). The shell-string SURFACE is still nominated, so no surface is missed, but verify.mjs:64 is NOT among the cited evidence — so the plan's factual claim is now false (CLAUDE.md rule 6: existing docs must stay truthful), and for a security tool, prose/self-fixture matches consuming the evidence budget ahead of real code sites is a precision regression.
findings.fix: Either correct the m3-plan.md note to state which real sites are actually cited, or improve precision so production code hits are not evicted by doc-prose matches (e.g. de-prioritize .md files for the shell-string line regex, or raise/rebalance the per-surface evidence cap).

findings.issue: The self-host bundle manifest still stamps veriloop_version 0.3.7 while VERILOOP_VERSION and the six checked stamps moved to 0.3.8. This is a self-host bundle artifact (not one of the six enumerated stamps, and lint-bundle passes), so it does not fail the gate, but it is stale drift that a self-host regen would surface. Drift-owned; flagged only for visibility.
findings.fix: Regenerate the self-host bundle (or leave until the M3 0.4.0 milestone stamp lands, per spec §7) — confirm this is the intended deferral, not an oversight.

lenses.summary: Plan/spec fidelity is high and the selftest is green (224 ok, 212→224 growth). I verified the load-bearing invariants hold: no LLM and zero spawns inside scan.mjs (constitution rules 2/4/5), the scan-target fixture is read as TEXT and never executed (the selftest interrogates scan's DECISION, not fixture output — rule 3), the classification-confirm halt writes-then-exits 0 without chaining into mining, the six version stamps all agree at 0.3.8, and scan.mjs is correctly compiler-side (absent from the emitted manifest; lint-bundle stays green). A live smoke of scan against veriloop itself correctly surfaced verify.mjs shell:true, the .gitignore splice markers, and the golden-fixture surfaces. One real correctness flaw: the two spec-required properties BOUNDED (--max) and RESUMABLE (path cursor) are mutually destructive — capping surfaces permanently drops the un-emitted ones because the cursor marks ALL walked paths scanned regardless of the cap. Latent under the default (max 12 > 7 catalog entries) but a data-loss footgun the moment --max is set below the number of hitting surfaces, which is exactly what --max is for. Two minor doc-drift nits.

findings.issue: BOUNDED (--max) and RESUMABLE (scanned_paths cursor) are mutually destructive. The emit loop stops after --max NEW surface blocks (line 247), but the cursor merge unconditionally marks EVERY walked path as scanned (line 257: `for (const p of allPaths) scannedPaths.add(...)`). On a re-run, `fresh` (line 238) is empty because all paths are in the cursor, so any surface that was capped out on the first invocation can never be emitted — it is dropped permanently, not deferred. The spec lists both BOUNDED and RESUMABLE as first-class required behaviors; their intended combination is incremental progress across invocations, not silent permanent loss. Reproduced against veriloop itself: `scan --repo . --out /tmp/vlm.md --max 2` emits 2 surfaces and writes 73 paths to the cursor; a follow-up `--max 12` run reports `0 new this run` and the 3 remaining real surfaces (DB/SQL/RLS, filesystem writes, parity/golden) are never emitted. Latent under the default (max 12 > 7 catalog entries) so the acceptance path passes, but --max is a data-loss footgun in exactly the scenario it exists for.
findings.fix: Decouple the cursor from the cap: only mark a path as scanned once ALL its surfaces have been emitted, OR make --max bound paths/evidence processed per invocation (advancing the cursor by that many paths) rather than post-hoc capping emitted blocks. Minimal alternative: when `emitted >= args.max` truncates emission, do NOT add the still-unscanned paths to the cursor so the next run re-collects them. Add a selftest that caps below the surface count and asserts a second run emits the remainder.

findings.issue: The self-hosted bundle manifest still stamps `veriloop_version: 0.3.7` while the six canonical stamps moved to 0.3.8. This is consistent with the spec's decision to leave the self-host bundle unregenerated for this slice (scan.mjs is not emitted), so it is not a gate violation — but the committed self-host bundle now lags the compiler by one patch.
findings.fix: Acceptable per spec (self-host bundle regenerates when M3's exit criteria land at 0.4.0). No action required for this slice; noted so the lag is intentional, not forgotten.

findings.issue: The added implementation note claims 'the SKILL.md:103 reference to scan-notes.md is now truthful.' SKILL.md phase 3 describes the LLM-driven dev-loop scan writing to `$REPO/.claude/veriloop/scan-notes.md`; deterministic scan.mjs defaults to stdout and its acceptance/selftest write to /tmp — it does not itself write that path by default. The two mechanisms coexist; the note slightly conflates them.
findings.fix: Soften the note to state that scan.mjs is the deterministic counterpart of the SKILL.md phase-3 flow, rather than asserting the SKILL.md path reference is fulfilled by this script.

implPreflight: npm test (the repo's only gate; this project is deliberately dependency-free, so there is no separate lint/typecheck) exit 0 — 224 ok, 0 failed (grew from the 212 baseline). node -c on scripts/scan.mjs and scripts/selftest.mjs exit 0. node scripts/lint-bundle.mjs --bundle <generated> exit 0 (scan.mjs is compiler-side, not emitted; 0 in bundle, 0 in manifest). Acceptance: scan --repo fixtures/scan-target --out /tmp/sn.md exit 0, schema + :line citations present, second run headers==unique (no duplicate headers).
